Text:
Main aapko pichhle 5 saalon se lagatar YouTube pe subscribe karke rakha hoon. Aapke videos aur mobile se judi jaankari se mujhe bahut kuch seekhne ko mila hai. Aap har phone ka review seedhi aur sachchi bhasha mein samjhate ho, isliye aap par poora bharosa hai. Main Tufanganj Dist. Coochbehar se hun. Main chahta hun ki main bhi ek achha smartphone use karun. Abhi mere pas 7 saal purana "MIA3" phone hain. Mujhe Iphone isliye pasand hai kyunki lagbhag mere sare dosto ke pas Iphone hain lekin mere pas nhi hain kyuki meri family mujhe ye afford karke nhi de sakte. Or iphone main hamara privacy ka dhyan rakkha jata hain, or iphone ka camera or performance dono acche hain.

Pichle video pe bhi maine comment Kiya tha lekin mujhe nhi mila .....Agar aap mujhe ye Iphone gift kar dete ho, to ye mere liye bahut badi khushi ki baat hogi or mere dost mera 7 saal purana phone bolke majak bhi nhi udayenge. Main hamesha aapko support karta rahunga aur aapke content ko aage bhi share karta rahunga.

Jai baba ki

I have been keeping you subscribed on YouTube continuously for the last 5 years. I have learned a lot from your videos and mobile information. Now I have a 7 year old "MIA3" phone. I like Iphone because almost all my friends have Iphone but I don't have one because my family can't afford it. Or the iPhone mainly takes care of our privacy, or the camera and performance of the iPhone are both good.

I also commented on the previous video but I did not get it….If you gift me this iPhone, it will be a matter of great happiness for me or my friend will not even make fun of my 7 year old phone. I will always support you and will continue sharing your content in future also.

Text:
Personally, as an iPhone 16 user, being involved with the tech community and having owned a repair business - I'm switching TO the 2 year old Pixel 9 Pro, Because software, hardware features and unlocked bootloaders for more secure operating systems make allll the difference. iOS 26 has been the worst experience I've ever had on a smartphone since the Windows Lumia 550

The power of the A18 chip just is not good enough to outweigh all the terrible, poorly optimized and feature lacking GUI of modern iPhones. I don't even trust iOS security anymore.

The only viable purpose of judging a high performing phone is if you plan to keep it beyond its lifespan, which no one usually does because people who can't afford high performing phones don't buy them to begin with, those who do just upgrade long before they lose support or slow down.   Optimisation and features outweighs CPU performance by a mile if it's powerful enough to remain strong for as long as the security updates
Translated:

Text: Yo, my personal beef with this new iPad mini is, I'm a total gaming bro, dude. And this like 8-inch gadget? Fuck, it's the perfect size for me to carry around or game on. Of course, the iPad's got that badass A17 Pro chip – super powerful, runs games buttery smooth. Plus the optimization kicks ass compared to those other tablets. But why the hell they stick a 60Hz screen on it? Come on, gimme 90 or 120! The CPU's got the chops, but it's bottlenecked by the damn display refresh rate. Just imagine blasting Genshin Impact at 120 FPS on an iPad mini while you're chilling on the john – that'd be next-level bliss. But nah, you're stuck with 60Hz and 60 FPS. What pisses me off is, even if I bail to some Chinese tablet ready to hug me with 120-144Hz, you know damn well barely any games support unlocking 90-120 FPS on Android tabs – especially the ones I love like Honkai Star Rail or Genshin Impact. No matter how beastly your Snapdragon 8 Elite is, it's capped at 60 FPS anyway.
Translated: Yo, my personal beef with this new iPad mini is, I'm a total gaming bro, dude. And this like 8-inch gadget? Fuck, it's the perfect size for me to carry around or game on. Of course, the iPad's got that badass A17 Pro chip – super powerful, runs games buttery smooth. Plus the optimization kicks ass compared to those other tablets. But why the hell they stick a 60Hz screen on it? Come on, gimme 90 or 120! The CPU's got the chops, but it's bottlenecked by the damn display refresh rate. Just imagine blasting Genshin Impact at 120 FPS on an iPad mini while you're chilling on the john – that'd be next-level bliss. But nah, you're stuck with 60Hz and 60 FPS. What pisses me off is, even if I bail to some Chinese tablet ready to hug me with 120-144Hz, you know damn well barely any games support unlocking 90-120 FPS on Android tabs – especially the ones I love like Honkai Star Rail or Genshin Impact. Text: I just picked up a 2021 M1 Pro 16 512 for around $1100. It came with a magic keyboard, a magic mouse and apple care until 2026. Hands down the best investment I have ever made into my productivity. I am actually now considering selling my desktop set up because of of good the laptop is. If you are considering getting a MacBook Pro, the M1 Pro is a really good value proposition right now. Even comparing against the M3promax machines, I can't justify the extra spend even with the performance increase. Everyone seems to really love their M1 machines. I might consider upgrading to the M4pro max when it comes out but that's later down the line. If you are on the fence I would go for it, there are some deals to be had out there for sure.
Translated: I just picked up a 2021 M1 Pro 16 512 for around $1100. It came with a magic keyboard, a magic mouse and apple care until 2026. Text: Если рассматривать с точки зрения максимального увеличения, то 1 + 12 выигрывает. Но если рассматривать с точки зрения качества фото, то необходимо было их делать в одинаковом максимальном (минимальном увеличении одного из представленных смартфонов) разрешении. То есть взять Айфон 15 про макс с х25 и поставить Самсунг Гелакси С 25 ультра, а так же 1+ 12 в том же увеличении х25 и сделать снимки. После их сравнить между собой. Автор либо симпатизирует яблоку, т.к. предоставляет именно фото смартфонов в максимальном разрешении и намекая на качество снимков, либо показывает, что андроиды могут зумить больше. А если сравнивать фото в разрешении х25, то становится понятно, что разницу придётся рассматривать под микроскопом. При этом 1+ 12 стоит примерно в 2 раза меньше.
Translated: When viewed in terms of maximum magnification, 1 + 12 wins. But if we consider it from the point of view of photo quality, then it was necessary to take them in the same maximum (minimum magnification of one of the presented smartphones) resolution. That is, take an iPhone 15 pro max with x25 and install a Samsung Galaxy S 25 ultra, as well as 1+ 12 at the same magnification x25 and take pictures. Then compare them with each other. The author either sympathizes with the apple, because... provides photos of smartphones in maximum resolution and hints at the quality of the pictures, or shows that androids can zoom more. And if you compare photos in x25 resolution, it becomes clear that the difference will have to be examined under a microscope. At the same time, 1+ 12 costs about 2 times less.
